stories.headline: US navy seizes Iranian ship after it breaches blockade, says Trump
stories.summary: Action comes hours after president said he was sending officials to Pakistan for fresh peace talks with Tehran

stories.headline: UAE in Talks With US for Possible Financial Lifeline, WSJ Says
stories.summary: The United Arab Emirates has begun talks with the US about a financial backstop in case the Iran war plunges the country into further crisis, the Wall Street Journal reported, citing US officials it didn’t identify.

stories.headline: Trump says US seized Iran-flagged ship trying to get past Hormuz blockade
stories.summary: US president says his country's ​forces ⁠stopped cargo ⁠ship Touska by 'blowing a hole' in ‌its engine room.

stories.headline: Pope Leo tells Angola during huge Mass to ‘build hope’
stories.summary: On the third leg of his Africa tour, Leo urges Angola to move beyond "divisions" created by its war-scarred past.

stories.headline: US intercepts and seizes Iranian-flagged cargo ship, Trump says
stories.summary: Tehran has not commented on the incident, which comes as the US prepares for a second round of talks.

stories.headline: Ukraine police chief resigns after officers allegedly fled deadly shooting
stories.summary: Authorities say the officers have been suspended and an investigation into their actions is under way.

stories.headline: Rat poison found in HiPP baby food jar in Austria, police say
stories.summary: The brand warned on Saturday that consuming the purées may be potentially "life-threatening".

stories.headline: Zelensky condemns US extension of Russian sanctions waiver
stories.summary: The US argues that the waiver is meant to ease the energy supply crunch sparked by the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.headline: How Iran has been studying lessons from the war in Ukraine
stories.summary: Military journals provide tantalising glimpses into what Tehran’s military thinks and its priorities, including drones

stories.headline: BlackRock warns of hit to European stocks from energy crisis
stories.summary: World’s biggest asset manager had been more bullish on the region at start of year but says stocks are no longer cheap

stories.headline: Trump Says US Military Seized An Iranian Vessel in Gulf of Oman
stories.summary: US Navy ship stopped Iranian-flagged cargo ship in its tracks “by blowing a hole in the engineroom” and US marines have custody of the ship, President Donald Trump says on Truth Social.

stories.headline: US Officials Head to Pakistan Again Amid Hormuz Standoff
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump said he’ll send representatives to Pakistan for talks to end the Iran war on Monday evening, while again threatening to strike civilian infrastructure if Tehran doesn’t agree to a deal. Axios White House Reporter Marc Caputo and Atlantic Staff Writer Nancy Youssef join David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Rep. Stutzman: Iran War Has Made Life Harder For Constituents
stories.summary: Indiana Congressman Marlin Stutzman (R) joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss recent statements from President Trump. The President said he’ll send representatives to Pakistan for talks to end the Iran war on Monday evening, while again threatening to strike civilian infrastructure if Tehran doesn’t agree to a deal. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: EU Special Rep. Ollogren Says Peace Requires Israel Restrains Itself
stories.summary: European Union Special Representative for Human Rights Kajsa Ollongren joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend the upcoming European Leaders Summit as they meet on Friday for talks with the war in Iran on the agenda. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Ukraine targeted with over 230 Russian drones, Zelenskyy blasts US green light for Russian oil
stories.summary: Ukrainian Defence Forces targeted drone manufacturing plants in Russia and on occupied Ukrainian territory.

stories.headline: In Angola, love for an American pope but not for an American president
stories.summary: In Luanda, Catholics expressed love and admiration for Pope Leo XIV, the first American pope, but not so much for President Trump because of the war in Iran.

stories.headline: Party of Bulgaria’s pro-Russian former president ahead in exit polls
stories.summary: Rumen Radev’s centre-left Progressive Bulgaria group ran on anti-corruption platform in country’s eighth election in five yearsThe party of Bulgaria’s pro-Russia former president Rumen Radev has come first in the country’s eighth parliamentary elections in five years, according to exit polls, but without securing a majority.Radev, who resigned as president in January, ran on a pledge to fight corruption after an anti-graft movement triggered a lo

stories.headline: Schools, shops shut in northern Israel to protest the Lebanon ceasefire
stories.summary: Shops and schools shut in northern Israel as residents protested a 10-day ceasefire with Lebanon.

stories.headline: Two police officers suspended after fleeing Kyiv shooting scene
stories.summary: An inquiry was opened after a video showed officers fleeing in panic from Saturday’s shooting, which left six dead and many injured. The patrol police chief has resigned.

stories.headline: China hosts humanoid robots in Beijing half marathon
stories.summary: China has tested humanoid robots in a real race setting in Beijing. The event highlights advances in endurance, balance and AI behaviour. Engineers see it as a step towards wider use.

stories.headline: Pope says 'tyrants' speech was not aimed at Trump
stories.summary: The pontiff says his remarks have been misinterpreted after a spat with the US president.

stories.headline: Australia's most-decorated soldier vows to fight war crime charges
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith has given his first statement since he was charged with five counts of the war crime of murder last week.

stories.headline: Watch: Runners v robots at China half marathon
stories.summary: Robots competed in a half marathon race in Beijing on Sunday, with the winning machine leaving its human rivals for dust.

stories.headline: Traders Brace for Renewed Turmoil on Hormuz Standoff
stories.summary: Traders are preparing for a bumpy open to the week as a continued standoff around the Strait of Hormuz revives the uncertainty that Wall Street had been eager to look past, after a week that drove the S&P 500 Index to a record and pushed oil back toward $90 a barrel.

stories.headline: Option Traders Chasing Torrid Stock Rally Turn Focus to Earnings
stories.summary: Option traders have flipped from concern that the Iran war will sink the economy to fear of missing out on the torrid equity rebound. That shift away from macro risk to individual stocks is set to continue as corporate earnings take center stage.

stories.headline: What to Expect From Fed Chair Nominee Kevin Warsh's Confirmation Hearing
stories.summary: The Senate Banking Committee has scheduled a confirmation hearing at 10 a.m. on April 21 for Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump’s nominee for Federal Reserve chair. Bloomberg News Washington Correspondent Tyler Kendall and Bloomberg News International Economics and Policy Correspondent Michael McKee join David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Ben Roberts-Smith denies war crimes allegations – As it happened

stories.headline: Rat poison found in baby food jar in Austria after product recall
stories.summary: Police say poison detected in jar of HiPP carrots and potatoes as maker says items may have been tampered withRat poison has been found in a jar of HiPP baby food, police in Austria have said, after a recall of the product from more than 1,000 Spar supermarkets in the country over safety fears.Police in Burgenland said in a statement that a sample from one of the 190g (7oz) jars of carrots and potatoes baby food reported by a customer had tested

stories.headline: Exit poll shows former President Radev’s party set to win Bulgaria election
stories.summary: Radev's Progressive Bulgaria party projected to win 38.1 percent of the vote in parliamentary election.

stories.headline: Peru says presidential election results due by mid-May after delayed count
stories.summary: The EU’s election observer said the vote met democratic standards despite fraud allegations.

stories.headline: Pope Leo XIV heads to shrine in Angola that was a center of African slave trade
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV headed to a Catholic shrine in Angola that was a center of African slave trade. Earlier, the pontiff celebrated Mass before an estimated 100,000 people.

stories.headline: Drone footage shows huge Malaysian coastal village fire
stories.summary: Thousands of people have been displaced after a fire destroyed around 1,000 homes in Malaysia's Sabah state.

stories.headline: The stock market’s new approach to valuation
stories.summary: It is demonstrating an Earnings Before Iran, Tariffs and Dubious Announcements (ebitda) mentality

stories.headline: Starmer battles for Downing Street future amid Mandelson vetting scandal
stories.summary: Prime minister’s statement to MPs will come ahead of testimony from sacked Foreign Office head Olly Robbins

stories.headline: Wealth advisers made more than $2bn from private capital fees
stories.summary: FT analysis of 16 funds shows extent of fees paid to banks and brokerages

stories.headline: Hedge Fund Pay Spirals Even Higher in New Trader-Poaching Strategy
stories.summary: As the competition for talent reaches new extremes, ‘interception trades’ between rivals are heating up.

stories.headline: Point72, BlackRock-Backed Contest Seeks Japanese Banking Talent
stories.summary: Scores of Japanese college students have been obsessing over sales of toothpaste, diapers and chocolate. They have visited supermarkets and drugstores to photograph shelves, scrutinized price tags and noted which brands sit at eye level for maximum exposure.

stories.headline: Bank Debate Interview: Umar Farooq and Shahmir Khaliq
stories.summary: Global Co-Head of JP Morgan Payments, Umar Farooq and Head of Services at Citi, Shahmir Khaliq, spoke with Bloomberg's Emily Mason in a bank debate interview. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: After Long Avoiding Politics, Commodity Traders Cozy Up to Trump
stories.summary: For decades, the world’s biggest commodity traders have sought to remain outside of politics, doing deals with anyone who has natural resources to buy or sell. Now, Donald Trump is changing the rules of the game.

stories.headline: Bloomberg This Weekend 4/19/2026
stories.summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by The Associated Press International Correspondent Philip Crowther, Blue Origin CEO Dave Limp, “The Blue Food Cookbook” Author, Chef & Restaurateur Andrew Zimmern, Former National Security Advisor Principal Deputy Jon Finer, EU Special Representative

stories.headline: Are the Rich Paying Their Fair Share of Taxes?
stories.summary: Taxes have long been at the center of debates over fairness, growth, and the role of government. Yale law professor Natasha Sarin argues that gaps in the system, including the treatment of capital gains and inherited wealth, allow significant income to go untaxed, limiting federal revenue at a time of rising deficits. Investor Steve Rattner says higher taxes may be unavoidable given fiscal pressures, while University of Richmond professor Jessica

stories.headline: Chef Andrew Zimmern Explains Eating for Sustainability
stories.summary: With Earth Day fast approaching, Chef and Restaurateur Andrew Zimmern joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the concept of 'blue foods,' sustainable food sourced from water such as fish, shellfish, and seaweed, and their critical role in combating climate change and addressing global hunger.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Emerging-Market Bond Sales Are Soaring Again as Investors Dive Back Into Risk
stories.summary: Emerging-market bond sales are roaring back from last month’s doldrums, as issuers from Brazil to Turkey take advantage of rebounding markets to raise fresh financing.

stories.headline: Trump Administration Demand for Ballot Info Rebuffed by Michigan
stories.summary: The Department of Justice is seeking ballots and other materials from the 2024 election. Michigan officials call it election interference.

stories.headline: Muslim Southerners Face a Fresh Wave of Hateful Political Rhetoric
stories.summary: Some Muslim voters were once drawn to Republican positions on family values and individual liberty, but as Southern politicians stoke anti-Islamic sentiment, many feel threatened.

stories.headline: Australia news live: Pocock buys billboards to pressure Chalmers on gas export tax; survey reveals national gloom
stories.summary: ACT independent David Pocock is calling for a 25% tax on gas exports to fund welfare and housing. stories.headline: Israeli ministers celebrate re-establishment of Sa-Nur West Bank settlement
stories.summary: Sa-Nur is one of four former West Bank settlements approved by the government two decades after settlers were evicted.

stories.headline: Japan's energy hunt runs into China's lead in Central Asia, Latin America
Kazakh gas sector comes of age, spurns Western partners for CITIC
Travel smart: Must-have gadgets and accessories for summer holidays in 2026
stories.summary: From power banks to travel pillows, these are the essential items you need to pack for smoother trips around Europe this summer.

stories.headline: Chernobyl's last wedding: The couple who married as a nuclear disaster unfolded
stories.summary: Serhiy and Iryna married 40 years ago, unaware a nuclear reactor had exploded less than 3 miles away.

stories.headline: Pope Leo XIV lands in Angola, says it is 'not in my interest at all' to debate Trump
stories.summary: After visiting Cameroon, Angola is the third leg of Pope Leo XIV's 11-day tour of Africa. People hope for appeals for peace and for him to tackle the economic woes of the oil- and rare-earth-rich nation.

stories.headline: At least six killed in Kyiv as gunman opens fire and takes hostages
stories.summary: The attacker, who took hostages inside a supermarket, was killed after a shoot-out with police, officials say.

stories.headline: French film star Nathalie Baye dies aged 77, media report
stories.summary: President Emmanuel Macron said France had "loved, dreamed and grown up" with the stalwart of French cinema.

stories.headline: Takeaways From the Supreme Court’s Shadow Papers
stories.summary: Confidential memos written by the justices shed light on how they came to issue emergency orders in cases about the scope of presidential power.

stories.headline: Why China is taking a behind-the-scenes role in the Iran war
stories.summary: As Trump pursued an unpopular war, Beijing positioned itself as a peacemaker, with little incentive to pressure Iran or annoy the U.S.

stories.headline: As sexual misconduct allegations rock Congress, some push for new rules
stories.summary: The resignations of two lawmakers facing ethics investigations have drawn comparisons to the #MeToo movement. Some House members say it’s time to revisit their rules and culture.

stories.headline: Spain's Sánchez builds anti-Trump coalition looking for political lifeline at home
stories.summary: Spanish PM led a progressive conference in Barcelona bringing together world leaders opposing MAGA politics, as Brazil's Lula lashed out at warlords and tech billionaires. "They're destroying democracy, workers and nature."

About 80% of the coalmines pumped

stories.headline: A moment of risk and opportunity: How two ceasefires could boost US-Iran talks
stories.summary: Some kind of diplomatic progress is now under way, but is it enough to prevent a return to war?

stories.headline: Ceasefire with Israel brings respite to Lebanon, but obstacles to peace remain
stories.summary: The halt to weeks of fighting is being celebrated in Lebanon, but it will be difficult to ensure it lasts.

stories.headline: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.summary: A 10-day truce between the two countries is now in effect, with Iran-backed Hezbollah voicing support, as negotiations continue between the US and Iran.

stories.headline: Harry and Meghan meet Bondi shooting survivors
stories.summary: On the fourth day of their Australian visit, the royal couple pay tribute to the 15 people killed in the Bondi shooting.

stories.headline: He made jazz under air raids - and built an Indian city's music scene
stories.summary: War correspondent, jazz bandleader and impresario, KC Sen shaped Kolkata’s music scene.

stories.headline: Chinese carmaker patents voice-controlled 'in-vehicle toilet'
stories.summary: Seres' plans show how stiff competition in the EV space is putting pressure on carmakers to innovate.

stories.headline: Major refinery fire won't lead to fuel rationing, Australian PM says
stories.summary: A 13-hour blaze at one of Australia's two oil refineries could impact petrol prices and supply.

stories.headline: How South Korea plans to use the Iran crisis to spur a renewables revolution
stories.summary: Energy crisis unfolding in Middle East has added political urgency, and more funding, to transform South Korea’s solar industryIn Guyang-ri, a farming village of 70 households about 90 minutes south-east of Seoul, people gather for communal free lunches six days a week. stories.headline: 'How does one survive?': Factory protests expose strain in India's industrial system
stories.summary: Workers in some north Indian cities have been protesting, demanding better pay and working conditions.

stories.summary: An office parable

stories.headline: Lyse Doucet: Under fragile ceasefire, Iranians wonder if US deal can be done
stories.summary: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.

stories.headline: Bongbong Marcos fights ill-health rumours with star jumps
stories.summary: The Philippine President challenged anyone questioning his fitness to join him in the gym.

stories.headline: Israelis war-weary but most oppose Iran ceasefire, poll suggests
stories.summary: The vast majority did not believe Hezbollah or Iran had been severely weakened by weeks of Israeli and US attacks, according to the poll.

stories.headline: How Trump’s Jesus-like image and feud with the Pope are sparking backlash
stories.summary: BBC's Sarah Smith looks at how the president's most ardent supporters are reacting to his latest social media posts.

stories.headline: 'A truly historic moment': Hungarian opposition wins election landslide
stories.summary: Rajini Vaidyanathan broadcasts from outside Hungary's parliament as crowds hear about the prime minister's concession.

stories.headline: Peru election drags into second day after ballot delivery fiasco
stories.summary: Technical and logistical problems mean Peruvians choosing a new president and members of Congress continue to vote on Monday.

stories.headline: How Hungary's knife-edge election could impact the US and Russia
stories.summary: The BBC's Europe Editor Katya Adler reports from Prime Minister Victor Orbán's home town of Felscút.
